c_signature: static void mpfr_sqrt2_approx (mpfr_limb_ptr rp, mpfr_limb_srcptr np)
doc:
PARKED. Performance-only sub-helper of mpfr_sqrt2 (mpfr/src/sqrt.c L33-L68). Puts an approximation of floor(sqrt(2^128 * n)) into the 2-limb pseudoregister rp[1]:rp[0], where n = np[1]*2^64 + np[0] is in [2^126, 2^128). C contract is the inequality {rp, 2} - 4 <= floor(sqrt(2^128*n)) <= {rp, 2} + 26 (mpfr/src/sqrt.c L29-L31). The output is then refined by mpfr_sqrt2's main loop (L390-L420) which uses mpn_sub_n / mpn_sqr fixup steps to tighten the approximation to the exact root.

ADR 0002 makes the parking criteria explicit. This helper meets criterion (i) — no public-API caller — so it stays parked irrespective of the inequality contract.

  1. **No public-API caller in the TS port (ADR 0002 criterion i).** The only C caller is mpfr_sqrt2 (the >64<128 prec window helper), which is also not selected for porting (state.db has no row for mpfr_sqrt2). The dispatcher in mpfr_sqrt routes here only as a performance fast path; the unified TS mpfr_sqrt (src/ops/sqrt.ts) uses a bigint isqrt across all precs and never delegates. Porting sqrt2_approx standalone would be dead code in `src/`.

  2. **Wire form misaligned with bigint TS surface (ADR 0002 criterion ii, weak).** Inputs are `mpfr_limb_srcptr np` (a raw 2-limb mp_limb_t array, little-endian per GMP); output is the same shape `mpfr_limb_ptr rp`. The golden-driver-substitute pattern (see mpfr_div2_approx) could express this as {np1, np0} -> {rp1, rp0} bigint limb tuples — but that's only worth doing once the parent mpfr_sqrt2 is selected and criterion (i) is resolved.

Note on the inequality contract: an earlier version of this spec listed the inequality as an independent reason for parking. ADR 0002 retired that reason. The golden-driver-substitute pattern (live in mpfr_div2_approx — composite=1.0 / 129 cases) shows that inequality-contract C helpers grade fine under strict equality: the golden_driver.c implements a portable substitute, the TS port mirrors the substitute, and both produce the same bit-exact output by construction. Inequality grading is structurally unneeded here.

Unblock condition (ADR 0002 §Revisit): mpfr_sqrt2 itself is selected for porting (which itself requires a perf-driven decision to break from the unified bigint-isqrt strategy in src/ops/sqrt.ts). At that point, port sqrt2_approx via golden-driver-substitute alongside the parent.
divergence_from_c:
PARKED  --  not ported. See doc field. Status note for state.db: status='blocked' with reason 'ADR 0002 criterion (i): no public-API caller in unified TS sqrt'.

ADR 0001 application  --  overrides documented even though no port ships:

  - signature.params: gen_spec emitted [rp, 'TODO: mpfr_limb_srcptr np'] because its parser stopped on the unrecognized mpfr_limb_srcptr type. Curator override: ['rp', 'np'], stripping the C type prefix per the standard naming convention. (ADR 0001: signature.params curator-wins.)
  - signature.returns: gen_spec emitted 'TODO: void'. Curator override: 'void' (no idiomatic-TS lift applies  --  there's nothing to lift to since the function is parked). (ADR 0001: signature.returns curator-wins.)
  - class: gen_spec did not assign (transcendental was the topo-rank inference); curator override to 'parked' for the state.db status filter. (ADR 0001: class curator-wins.)
  - prec_unit: gen_spec defaulted to 'bits'; curator override to 'n/a' (no prec parameter). (ADR 0001: prec_unit curator-wins on override.)
refs: mpfr/src/sqrt.c L29-L68 -- C reference body and inequality contract.
mpfr/src/sqrt.c L377-L425 -- mpfr_sqrt2's call site + refinement loop that tightens the approximation.
src/ops/sqrt.ts -- the unified TS mpfr_sqrt which obviates the need for prec-window helpers.
src/core.ts -- locked schema (no limb-array surface; bigint is the natural shape).
docs/adr/0001-spec-merge-policy.md -- per-field precedence overrides applied above.
docs/adr/0002-approximation-helper-grading.md -- the parking-criteria ADR; this spec is the canonical worked example.
eval/functions/mpfr_div2_approx/ -- live reference for the golden-driver-substitute pattern this spec defers to.
src/internal/mpfr/div2_approx.ts -- live reference for the TS port style under the substitute pattern.
docs/reports/011-shadow-trial-2.md -- the run that surfaced the framing gap ADR 0002 closes.
